pattern: if Ident { field: val }  // or while/for/match cond
fix: self/parser.hexa: add p_suppress_struct_lit flag set during parse_if_expr/parse_while_stmt/parse_for_stmt/parse_match_expr condition expression. parse_primary Ident branch skips struct-literal parsing when flag is set. Fixes `while c < N { ... }` where N is uppercase.

pattern: struct S { field }  // no type annotation
fix: self/parser.hexa parse_struct_decl: type annotation after field name now optional вАФ `field` and `field: int` both parse. Matches src/parser.rs convention.
self/parser.hexa parse_stmt @attr collection: attribute name may be a keyword token (Pure/Async/etc) вАФ grab tok.value directly instead of p_expect_ident which requires Ident.

pattern: fn foo(mut a: int, mut b: int)
workaround: drop mut, reassign locally inside fn body
fix: self/parser.hexa parse_params: accept optional Mut token before each parameter ident.

self/lexer.hexa: add PlusEq/MinusEq/StarEq/SlashEq/PercentEq tokens. parse_stmt: after parse_expr, detect compound-assign tokens and emit CompoundAssign{op,left,right} AST node. interpreter already handled CompoundAssign kind. Also applied the same tokens to parser.hexa's own self-contained tokenize() shadow function.

self/parser.hexa parse_primary: when Or token appears in expression position, treat as zero-arg Lambda (logical-or is never a primary). Falls back to Or operator when encountered mid-expression via higher-precedence parsers.

pattern: fn(x, y) { return x + y }  // anonymous fn expression
fix: self/parser.hexa parse_primary: add Fn branch вАФ consume 'fn', LParen, parse_params, RParen, optional Arrow+type, parse_block. Produces Lambda kind (params + body) compatible with existing closure machinery.
self/parser.hexa: add parse_invariant_stmt producing Invariant{left: expr}. parse_stmt dispatches Invariant token. interpreter exec_stmt_at: Invariant treated as runtime assertion (throw on false).

workaround: return value (incorrect вАФ doesn't unwind to effect call site)
fix: self/parser.hexa: add parse_resume_stmt, dispatched on Resume keyword. interpreter exec_stmt_at Resume: set resume_value + resume_set flag + return_flag to halt handler body. eval_effect_call consumes resume_value if set.

pattern: fn body: {let x = 1; x}  // last expr = return value
fix: interpreter.hexa call_user_fn: track last ExprStmt value in body loop. If no explicit return fired, use last_expr_val as return value. Matches Rust/Scala semantics.

workaround: wrap call in its own try/catch
fix: interpreter.hexa eval_expr_inner Call arm: check throw_flag/panic_flag after each arg evaluation. If set, return val_void() immediately so the enclosing call is not invoked and downstream `println('never')` is skipped. Mirrors src/interpreter.rs tight propagation.

pattern: fn dot(a, b) { ... }  // same name as builtin
fix: interpreter.hexa eval_call Ident path: check find_fn(name) before call_builtin. User functions shadow builtins cleanly. Fixes `fn dot` user definition being hijacked by the self-host `dot` (vector dot product) builtin.

interpreter.hexa eval_expr_at / eval_expr_inner IntLit/FloatLit branches: use string method parse_int() / parse_float() directly instead of user-defined to_int() / to_float() вАФ the latter expects a Val struct and crashes on host string .tag access.

self/parser.hexa parse_handle_stmt: accept Arrow/FatArrow/=> in handler body. interpreter: EffectDecl registers via node.items (was .ops), HandleWithStmt reads node.items and constructs key from h.target + '.' + h.name. effect_def_has_op and eval_effect_call use type_of 'array' check. eval_call Field branch routes Ident.op calls to eval_effect_call when Ident names a registered effect. resume statement sets resume_value + halts handler body.

interpreter.hexa: all internal char_code() calls updated from 1-arg (s[0]) to 2-arg (s, idx) form. self-host `for c in s.chars()` loops wrap c via to_string(c) so host char_code receives a string not a raw char element.
interpreter.hexa: replaced pi() calls with 3.14159265358979 literal. Affects cosine_lr, gelu approximation, randn (Box-Muller). Old host hexa binary does not expose pi as a callable builtin.

pattern: softmax(arr).sum()  // downstream usage of ML builtin output
fix: interpreter.hexa softmax: wrap each output element as val_float(e/sum_e) before val_array(out). Previously host floats were stored raw in array_store, crashing sum()/map() which expect items to be Val structs with .tag.

pattern: let x = err.to_string()  // Error Val + string method
fix: interpreter.hexa call_method: obj.tag == 'error' branch returns safe defaults for string methods (trim/to_lower/contains/split/bytes/chars/lines вЖТ '', 0, false, []). Prevents exec(...).trim() crash pattern on subprocess failure.

interpreter.hexa interpret() main loop: when return_flag set at top level, break out of the while loop (was reset-and-continue). Fixes auto_record_test pattern: if x { return }; let y = a[2]; // OOB because return was ignored.

pattern: src/ rust files fully absorbed into self/*.hexa

title: let mut x = 0 л≥АмИШ мШ§мЧЉ (val_int(0) мЇРмЛЬ singleton к≥µмЬ†)

note: val_int(0) returns shared singleton from __VAL_INT_CACHE. let mut stores the cached pointer directly via env_define, and downstream mutation corrupts the cache entry. Only let mut with initial value 0 is affected; immutable let and non-zero values are safe. Fix: 9591650 val_copy() for LetMutStmt. Workaround: init with non-zero then reassign.

summary: write_bytes used to_string(byte) вАФ emitted ASCII digits instead of raw bytes
wrong:
// interpreter write_bytes/write_file_bytes old path:
for b in arr { s = s + to_string(b.int_val) }  // 65 вЖТ "65", not chr(65)
right: for b in arr { s = s + from_char_code(b.int_val) }  // 65 вЖТ 'A'
note: Byte-array to string conversion must use from_char_code() to encode a byte value as its single-character representation. to_string() formats the integer as decimal digits, so writing [65,66] produced "6566" instead of "AB". WAV/PNG/weight dumps were scrambled without any error. Sister bug to P27 (fputs null truncation).

summary: call_builtin signature drift вАФ cosine_lr / batch_matvec / array_fill arg order mismatch
right:
// After b7cc2e9 the interpreter matches callers:
cosine_lr(base_lr, step, total[, min_lr])  // min_lr now optional
batch_matvec(W, X, out_dim, in_dim, batch)
array_fill(n, value)
note: The interpreter builtin table was the outlier вАФ callers had already standardized on a different order. This class of bug is invisible when argument types match (float/int blur): wrong answers (0 LR, transposed matmul, swapped fill) silently propagate. Always cross-check call_builtin arg indexing against at least two real call sites in self/ml/ or stdlib/optim.hexa before adding/modifying a builtin.

summary: slice(tensor, s, e) returned empty вАФ TAG_TENSOR missing from get_array dispatch
wrong:
let t = val_tensor([n], data)
let sub = slice(t, 0, 10)  // вЖТ []  because get_array(args[0]) rejects TAG_TENSOR
note: Tensors and arrays are distinct tags in the interpreter. Any builtin that needs indexable data must dual-dispatch; otherwise it silently returns an empty collection when fed a tensor. Pattern holds across matvec/batch_matvec/embedding/slice. Cross-check: grep `get_array(args\[0\])` and ensure nearby tensor branch exists.

summary: merged binary globals zero-initialized вАФ init_parts not separated from main_parts
note: Two-part regression: (1) globals must live in their own init function, (2) merged main() must call that init before any tokenize/parse/codegen work. Symptom after hexa cc --regen: hexa_v2 crashes or returns empty output from perfectly valid .hexa source. Affects both codegen_c2 and codegen_c2_full paths (keep synchronized).

summary: arg(0) / argc include binary name вАФ off-by-one vs native build
note: Silent divergence between `hexa run script.hexa foo` and the compiled binary run. Interpreter indexed host_args() from 0 (binary name) while native main() skipped argv[0] via hexa_set_args. CLI dispatch code checking arg(1) worked in one mode and broke in the other. Rule: test every CLI tool in both modes before shipping.

summary: read_file on missing path returns "" вАФ no error message, no exception
note: hexa_read_file returns hexa_str("") on fopen failure вАФ no OS error bubbles up. Empty-string and missing-file cases are indistinguishable without a pre-check. test_p13_error_fallback.hexa (3104391) codified the behavior: assertion 3 now expects msg=="", not a "No such file" substring. Callers expecting structured errors must gate on file_exists() or stat() first.

summary: nil / true / false мЛЭл≥ДмЮРл°Ь мЈ®кЄЙ вАФ C мљФлУЬмЧР кЈЄлМАл°Ь мґЬл†•лПЉ clang error
wrong:
let x = nil      // gen2_expr(Ident)к∞А кЈЄлМАл°Ь _hexa_mangle_ident("nil") л∞©мґЬ
// clang: error: use of undeclared identifier 'nil'
right:
// 365ae50: Ident лЕЄлУЬмЧРмДЬ nameмЭі nil/true/falseл©і
// hexa_void() / hexa_bool(1) / hexa_bool(0) мЭД л∞©мґЬ
note: Lexer emits nil/true/false as regular identifiers rather than literal tokens. The codegen identifier path must intercept by name before mangling. Easy to miss because interpreter path resolves these in the env via pre-bound globals. Any new codegen backend needs the same three-way check.

summary: stage0 interpreter evaluates all hex literals (0x7f, 0xff, 0x10) as 0 вАФ parse_int hard-coded base 10
note: Silent wrong-direction assertion bug: `elf[0] != 0x7f` became `elf[0] != 0` in interpret mode, so a correct ELF magic byte 0x7f triggered a FAIL/PASS flip depending on direction. Detection path was the ELF64 self-test in self/codegen/elf.hexa вАФ test appeared to pass but with wrong semantics. Verified after fix: test_elf64() still reports ALL CHECKS PASS but now with the assertions actually enforcing 127/69/76/70 magic bytes. Decimal, scientific notation, leading-zero decimal (0777вЖТ777, NOT octal 511), and negative hex (-0x10) all preserved. Rebuild: NO_SMOKE=1 hexa scripts/build_stage0.hexa after editing runtime.c вАФ note that scripts/rebuild_stage0.hexa has a separate bug in hash_of_files (parts.push is not in-place, so runtime.c hash is never actually computed) which causes the link cache to miss runtime.c-only changes.

summary: Val-arena (HEXA_VAL_ARENA=1 default) corrupts arrays returned from helper fn A and consumed in helper fn B вАФ len(arr) returns stale TAG_STR VALSTRUCT
note: Minimum reproducer (10 LOC): fn A returns `let mut c = []; c = c.push(x); return c`, fn B does `let r = A(); println(to_string(len(r)))`. Under HEXA_VAL_ARENA=1 (default since rt 32-L), len(r) returns a TAG_VALSTRUCT whose tag_i=TAG_STR(3), not TAG_INT. to_string() falls through the VALSTRUCT branch at runtime.c:2493 and emits 'Val{tag=3,i=<pointer>}'. With HEXA_VAL_ARENA=0, same code prints '1' correctly. Root cause likely in hexa_val_heapify() path for VALSTRUCT-wrapped TAG_ARRAY: the heapify walks array_store[idx] but does not re-anchor the caller's Val reference properly, and subsequent array_decref from env_pop_scope interacts with stale arena frontier. Affects the entire self/codegen/runtime_arm64.hexa self-test (exits 139/SIGSEGV under default run). P16 (argv()[N] NaN-box corruption) is the sibling class вАФ both manifest only with arena ON.
reproducer: /tmp/p40_detail.hexa вАФ fn inner returns let-mut pushed array, fn outer consumes via len()
workaround: HEXA_VAL_ARENA=0 for affected scripts; runtime_arm64.hexa header now documents this.

summary: immutable-array-push silently drops: `let a = []; a.push(x)` вАФ a stays empty because arrays are immutable and push() returns a new array
note: HEXA arrays are immutable; .push(x) returns a NEW array вАФ the result MUST be re-bound. Calling `a.push(x)` as a statement (result discarded) is silently a no-op. Compiler does not warn. Class of silent_bug that hits accumulator-loops hardest: the returned array is always empty, causing hashes/diffs/BFS queues/etc. to degenerate. Originally discovered via scripts/rebuild_stage0.hexa hash_of_files cache miss (fix 2527c70, 7 functions). Full codebase sweep on 2026-04-16 fixed 42 additional instances across 12 files: self/pgo.hexa (4), self/jit.hexa (4), self/escape_analysis.hexa (4), self/loop_unroll.hexa (3), self/work_stealing.hexa (3), self/dce.hexa (1), self/inline_cache.hexa (1), self/lsp.hexa (1), self/bench_suite.hexa (1), self/hexa_full.hexa (1), examples/self_host_lexer.hexa (11), examples/self_host_parser.hexa (6). Detection regex: `^\s*let\s+(?!mut\b)\w+\s*(?::[^=]+)?=\s*\[\s*\]` followed within 100 lines by `\bVAR\.push\s*\(` with no enclosing assignment.

summary: Bare struct/dict literal `{key: val}` not parseable вАФ use `#{"key": val}` dict syntax
wrong:
let r = {block: 1, offset: 2}
return {x: 10, y: 20}
right:
let r = #{"block": 1, "offset": 2}
// Field access still works: r.block == 1, r["offset"] == 2
note: Parser cannot disambiguate `{` as struct literal vs block start. The `#{}` prefix resolves this. Applies to all positions: top-level, inside if/while, function return, let binding. Discovered fixing arena_pure.hexa (2026-04-16).

summary: Function reassigning >4 global let mut variables corrupts slot table вАФ subsequent global reads/writes hit wrong slots
wrong:
let mut g0 = []
let mut g1 = []
let mut g2 = -1
let mut g3 = []
let mut g4 = []
let mut g5 = 0
let mut g6 = true
fn init() {
    g0 = []; g1 = []; g2 = -1; g3 = []; g4 = []; g5 = 0; g6 = true
}
init()  // corrupts slot table вАФ g0 becomes string, subsequent push fails
note: Stage0 interpreter (hexa_full.hexa) global scope env_set uses a slot index that drifts when many function definitions + many globals interact. Exact threshold varies (~6 globals + 6+ function definitions). Affects init/reset patterns that reassign many globals. The native codegen path (codegen_c2вЖТCвЖТclang) is NOT affected. Discovered during arena_pure.hexa testing (2026-04-16).

pattern: let x = 1e10; let y = 1.5e-3; let z = 2E5; let w = 9.99e30
fix: Lexer now consumes `e`/`E` followed by optional `+`/`-` and digits after any integer or float literal. The entire scientific notation form is emitted as a single FloatLit token. Examples: 1e10, 1.5e-3, 2E5, 9.99e30.

summary: Scientific notation literals (e.g. 9.99e30) require the exponent part to be consumed by the lexer вАФ stale stage0 binary silently drops the `e` suffix
wrong: let x = 9.99e30  // stale stage0: lexer stops at 9.99, `e30` becomes a separate identifier вЖТ wrong value (silent miscompilation)
right: let x = 9.99e30  // fixed lexer: entire literal consumed as FloatLit вЖТ 9.99e+30
note: G44. The lexer must consume `e`/`E` + optional sign + digits as part of the numeric literal. A stale stage0 binary that predates the fix will silently split the literal into a float (9.99) and an identifier (e30), producing wrong values without any error. Always rebuild stage0 after pulling the fix. Fixed 2026-04-17.

fix: codegen_c2 T34: detect top-level ExprStmt Call(Ident('main')) and suppress auto-appended u_main() in generated C main(). Bootstrap requires 2-pass: fix codegen_c2.hexa вЖТ rebuild_stage0 вЖТ hexa cc. Both codegen_c2() and codegen_c2_full() patched.

summary: fn main() + explicit main() call at module top-level вЖТ main body runs twice (T34)
note: T34. codegen_c2 emits `u_main()` in C main() for any file with `fn main()`. If the source also has a top-level `main()` ExprStmt, both fire. Fix: has_explicit_main_call flag. Bootstrap chicken-and-egg: fix must be manually patched into hexa_cc.c first, then stage0 rebuild propagates it. Fixed 2026-04-17.

summary: Generated C main() missing fflush(stdout) before return 0 вАФ pipe-connected stdout loses buffered output
note: When stdout is pipe-connected (remote dispatch, popen, ssh), it is fully buffered. Without fflush before return, output can be lost вАФ especially if the parent kills the child with SIGKILL (watchdog). Fixed in codegen_c2.hexa both codegen_c2() and codegen_c2_full(). Fixed 2026-04-17.

fix: codegen_c2 BinaryOp: when op is + or - and left operand is BinaryOp *, emit hexa_fma(a,b,c) instead of hexa_add(hexa_mul(a,b),c). CompoundAssign += with rhs BinaryOp * also emits hexa_fma. Runtime hexa_fma uses C99 fma() for floats (single rounding, hardware FMA), int fallback a*b+c. Saves one runtime dispatch call per fused site вАФ critical for dot products and weight updates.

description: Execute a shell command and capture both stdout and exit code. Returns a 2-element array: [stdout_string, exit_code_int]. Unlike exec(cmd) which discards the exit code and returns only stdout (or Error on failure), exec_with_status always returns a structured result вАФ exit_code != 0 means the command failed but stdout is still captured. Uses pclose() status in native codegen, shell $? sentinel trick in interpreter.

pitfalls.note: Same as P2: cmd must be a single string, not (cmd, [args]) form
Unlike exec(), exec_with_status does NOT throw on failure вАФ always returns array. No try/catch needed
Same as P8: beware PATH wrappers (~/.hx/bin/python3 etc.) вАФ use absolute paths for subprocess binaries

description: CUDA device memory pool manager. Bump-allocator arena over a single cudaMalloc chunk, eliminates per-tensor cudaMalloc/Free overhead during training.

Tensors that outlive a single step (weights, optimizer state) must use cuda_malloc/cuda_free from cuda_ffi.hexa, not the pool

Return next batch as [input_ids, target_ids] arrays of shape [batch_size, seq_len]. Returns nil at epoch end.
Fill prefetch ring buffer with n batches ahead of training step.

Current epoch number.
Progress within current epoch.
Loader metadata: total_tokens, vocab_size, n_sequences, batches_per_epoch.
Pack a flat array of token IDs into HXTD binary format.

DataLoader is immutable struct вАФ dl_prefetch/dl_reset_epoch return new DataLoader, must rebind: dl = dl_prefetch(dl, n)

description: Byte-level BPE tokenizer (GPT-2 style). Pure hexa implementation replacing Python tiktoken / HF tokenizers. Loads merges.txt + vocab.json.

Encode text string into token ID array using byte-level BPE.
Decode token ID array back to text string.
Batch encode multiple texts.
Return vocabulary size.

pitfalls.note: BPETokenizer is immutable вАФ bpe_load returns a new struct, no mutation methods
Byte-level encoding: non-ASCII UTF-8 bytes are mapped to unicode 256+ range (GPT-2 convention). Merges file must match this encoding.

summary: Pure Hexa ARM64/x86_64 encoders, Mach-O wrapper, IRвЖТARM64 bridge, zero external assembler. Added G46..G50 + P47..P49.

pattern:
let w = encode_mov(rd, rn)
code = emit32(code, w)
fix: ARM64 machine-code emission lives in self/codegen/arm64.hexa as pure-Hexa inline encoder functions (encode_mov / encode_rrr / encode_mul / encode_div / encode_msub / encode_svc / emit_mov_imm / emit32). Each returns an int (32-bit opcode) or extends a byte array; callers assemble by concatenation. No external assembler (as/clang -c) is shelled out вАФ the entire IRвЖТARM64 path is a pure-data pipeline that produces raw byte arrays suitable for Mach-O / ELF packaging. Same policy on x86_64: self/codegen/x86_64.hexa exposes x86_emit_mov_rr / x86_emit_alu_rr / x86_emit_mov_imm64 / x86_emit_syscall_insn etc.

pattern:
svc #0x80   // Darwin ARM64
svc #0      // Linux ARM64
syscall     // Linux x86_64
fix: ARM64 syscall trap is SVC with a platform-specific immediate encoded in bits [20:5] of 0xd4000001 (see encode_svc in self/codegen/arm64.hexa): Darwin uses svc #0x80, Linux uses svc #0. The syscall number register also differs вАФ x16 on Darwin, x8 on Linux (emit_arm64_syscall_nr picks by os flag). Arg registers x0..x7 are shared. On x86_64 Linux the trap is the `syscall` instruction (0x0F 0x05, x86_emit_syscall_insn), syscall number in rax, args in rdi/rsi/rdx/r10/r8/r9. Darwin numbers are the BSD table + 0x2000000 mask (write=0x2000004, exit=0x2000001); Linux AArch64 numbers are the unistd table (write=64, exit=93); Linux x86_64 uses the x86_64 unistd table (write=1, exit=60).

Pure-Hexa Mach-O emission in self/codegen/macho.hexa + self/compile_hello.hexa: (1) 16384-byte __TEXT page containing mach_header_64 + load commands (LC_SEGMENT_64 __PAGEZERO, LC_SEGMENT_64 __TEXT, LC_SEGMENT_64 __LINKEDIT, LC_MAIN, LC_CODE_SIGNATURE, LC_BUILD_VERSION). (2) Code bytes follow at file offset 16384 inside the __TEXT segment. (3) __LINKEDIT reserves space for the codesign blob. (4) compile_hello.hexa writes hex via printf|xxd -r -p, chmods +x, then shells out `codesign --force --sign - <path>` for ad-hoc signing (required on Apple Silicon). Result: hello-world native Mach-O emitted entirely by Hexa, clang=0, as=0.

self/codegen/ir_to_arm64.hexa regalloc_linear_simple maps SSA value ids 0..7 directly to ARM64 general-purpose regs x0..x7. Values with id >= 8 are flagged with sentinel 31 (XZR); the bridge emits `MOVZ x0, #0xDEAD` at those sites so callers notice unsupported spills rather than silently mis-compiling. regalloc_is_spill(map, vid) is the predicate. This keeps the bridge simple for the P7-6 / P7-7 breadth push; real linear-scan with stack slots lives in self/codegen/regalloc_linear.hexa for later scale-up. IR op instruction footprints are fixed (see ira_ops_size) so branch/jump offsets can be resolved in the 2-pass emit scheme.

pattern:
// IR Stmt вЖТ ARM64 lowering coverage
Return(e?) | Break | Continue | While(c,b) | Match(e,arms) | IfExpr(c,t,e)
fix: self/ir/lowering.hexa extended from the P7-5 Expr-only subset to full Stmt coverage: Return (with/without value), Break, Continue, While (header-block + body + back-edge), Match (desugared to a chain of IfExpr on each arm's pattern test), IfExpr as rvalue (produces a phi'd Value), plus supporting machinery for labelled loop exits. This closes the gap between the parsed AST and the IR that ir_to_arm64.hexa consumes, so the native backend can now compile programs with nontrivial control flow (fib, match dispatch, while counters). Expr-side extensions from the same session: BoolLit, UnaryOp, BinOp recursion, Assign, ForRange (see commit 6249f88).

summary: Mach-O codesign replacement warning вАФ stale signature from template must be overwritten, not appended
note: G48. The 16384-byte Mach-O template reserves an LC_CODE_SIGNATURE slot in __LINKEDIT. If the template was copied from a previously-signed binary, the old signature hash no longer matches the new __TEXT bytes and the loader rejects the binary on Apple Silicon. `codesign --force --sign -` overwrites with an ad-hoc signature computed from the actual __TEXT. Caught by self/codegen_native_fib.hexa when its output refused to execute until we added the resign step. Always resign on every emit; check exit code.

summary: ADR pc-relative displacement must be recomputed whenever data layout shifts вАФ do NOT hard-code the offset
note: ARM64 ADR encodes a 21-bit signed pc-relative displacement split across two immediate fields. Any change that moves either the ADR site or the target (extra load command, different code size from regalloc decisions, more string literals) invalidates the offset. Must be resolved in the same 2-pass scheme used for branch targets (see P49). Mitigation: keep all literal data in a single __cstring section at the tail of __TEXT, and compute displacements only after pass-1 sizing is finalized.

summary: 2-pass offset resolution required for branch/jump targets вАФ op sizes vary (e.g. sub=1 vs sub+cset=2, load_imm=1 vs movz+movk=2)
note: Single-pass emission is a classic native-codegen trap: any op whose size depends on operand values (immediate width, compare fusion, large-constant materialization) makes forward-branch offsets unknowable until the whole function is sized. self/codegen/ir_to_arm64.hexa solves this with explicit ira_ops_size + two passes; self/codegen/mod.hexa extends the same idea across functions via call_fixups (BL patched after all functions are laid out). x86_64 CALL (E8 rel32) gets the same fixup treatment (patch_call_fixups_x86).

symptom: shift-op tokens `<<` and `>>` silently rejected in expression positions within multi-operand expressions like `(r << 1) | (x & 1)` or `x >> 1`. Parse error is printed at CLI but execution proceeds and the expression yields 0 вАФ silent correctness hazard. `&` works fine; only `<<`/`>>` are fragile.
detection: Parse error message printed to stdout/stderr at CLI invocation, but process exit code = 0 (no failure propagated). Result value is a bogus 0 вАФ downstream assertions silently pass with wrong data. Confirmed by numeric/math batch agent in commit 7873f76.
fix_status: OPEN вАФ parser regression despite grammar claim (G40/G41/G42 listed status=fixed_pending_rebuild, but stage0 surface parser does not honor the fix in multi-operand expression positions)
workaround: Use pow2(n) via * 2 / / 2 plus disjoint-bit addition. Reference implementations: self/runtime/bit_pure.hexa (bit_shl_pure via a * pow2(n)); self/runtime/bitops_pure.hexa (bit_reverse32_pure uses * 2 / / 2 + disjoint-bit composition).

context: using a reserved keyword as a variable, parameter, or field identifier in hexa source (e.g. `let mut borrow = 0`, `fn f(handle: int)`, `fn sub(a, b) { let mut borrow = 0; ... }`)
symptom: Variable/parameter silently binds to `void` or becomes a no-op. NO parse error emitted. Arithmetic against it yields `[void, void]` / void / 0 results downstream. Encountered in bignum_mini_pure's bignum_sub_pure where `let mut borrow = 0` silently produced `[void, void]` for every subtraction step.
detection: At runtime: `to_string(x) == "void"` or `type_of(x) == "void"` where a numeric value was expected. Output arrays contain `void` entries instead of computed results. No CLI error, no parse error message вАФ purely silent miscompilation.
reserved_source: self/lexer.hexa is_keyword() (also mirrored in self/hexa_full.hexa, self/native/parser_v2.c, self/token.hexa). Ownership DSL (own/borrow/move/drop), concurrency DSL (spawn/channel/select/atomic), effect DSL (effect/handle/resume), proof DSL (pure/proof/assert/invariant/theorem), meta DSL (macro/derive/where/comptime/intent/generate/verify/optimize), scope (scope/defer/typeof/guard/with) are ALL reserved even though most are not yet actively parsed вАФ they silently shadow when used as identifiers.
fix_status: PITFALL (user-side workaround вАФ rename identifier). Parser SHOULD emit an error when a reserved keyword is used in let/fn-param/field position, but currently does not. No fix TODO filed; workaround is authoritative.
workaround: Rename the identifier. Common substitutions: borrow -> br / carry / borrow_flag, handle -> hdl / h / hnd, own -> owner / owned, move -> mv / moved, drop -> dropped / drop_fn, pure -> is_pure, guard -> guard_cond, with -> ctx, scope -> scope_name, select -> choice, where -> clause.

summary: @select(family, cond -> algo, ..., else -> algo) вАФ meta-DSL attribute that declares a runtime algorithm-family dispatch rule on a fn decl (S-2c milestone).
syntax_ebnf: '@' 'select' '(' family_name (',' select_arm)* ')' \n select_arm := ( expr | 'else' ) '->' algo_ident \n family_name := string_literal | char_literal | ident
example_canonical: @select("sort", n <= 16 -> insertion, n <= 10000 -> quick, else -> merge)\nfn sort_dispatch(xs) { return xs }
example_multi_arm: @select("matmul", m * n <= 4096 -> naive_mm, m * n <= 262144 -> blocked_mm, else -> blis_mm)
parse_serialization: p_pending_annotations stores 'select|FAMILY@@COND_RAW~>ALGO@@COND_RAW~>ALGO@@else~>DEFAULT'. Arms are in source order; '~>' separates cond from algo; '@@' separates arms; 'else' literal marks the default arm.
parser_site: self/parser.hexa parse_stmt() @attr loop вАФ dedicated branch ahead of the @cli/@flag raw-capture branch. Generic-attribute fallback (paren-balanced skip) continues to accept the form without error even on stage0 binaries that predate this branch (forward-compatible).
consumer_site: S-2d (pending) вАФ self/opt/algorithm_select.hexa will lower the serialized annotation into an if-cascade over registered algorithms. family_name keys into shared/hexa-lang/algorithm_registry (S2-1 DONE).
ast_impact: No new AST field. FnDecl.annotations accumulates 'select|...' strings alongside existing @cli/@flag/@doc/@schema/@gate/@memo entries (self/ast.hexa FnDecl.annotations).
fallback: A grammar.jsonl-only landing is acceptable progress if the parser patch cannot be merged safely. The current commit lands both the parser branch AND this spec row.

context: @select(family, cond -> algo, ..., else -> algo) meta-DSL on fn decls (S-2c)
symptom: On stage0 binaries built before the S-2c parser patch, the @select attribute is silently accepted by the generic-attribute fallback (paren-balanced skip) вАФ the form parses, the host fn runs, but NO structured rule is captured in FnDecl.annotations. This is intentional forward-compat (no source break), but callers inspecting FnDecl.annotations will see an empty 'select|...' entry until stage0 is rebuilt via scripts/rebuild_stage0.hexa (HX8).
detection: After adding @select(...) on a fn, run stage0 and check that annotations contain a 'select|FAMILY@@...' entry. If absent but the file still parses, the binary predates S-2c.
known_reserved_interaction: The `select` keyword is reserved (see P51) for the concurrency `select {}` stmt. Using it as the attribute name `@select` is safe because the parser's @attr loop accepts any keyword token's value as the attribute name (parser.hexa L461-465). Do NOT rename the host fn to `select` вАФ only the `@select(...)` attribute form is special-cased.
